phases.name: Schema + migration: denormalize project_id onto subtype tables

steps.name: Add immutable project_id column + index to thought.schema.ts
criteria: thoughts table exports a projectId column ('project_id', uuid, notNull) referencing entities.id onDelete cascade
thoughts table declares a btree index 'idx_thoughts_project_id' on project_id
header comment reframes project_id as a denormalized immutable copy of the entity's project scope, dropping the old 'No project_id' note
column/index shape matches the reference pattern in chunk.schema.ts and relationship.schema.ts exactly
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: static schema metadata, not runtime behavior; backfill-correctness property compensates at step 01-03
steps.scenario_name: thoughts table exposes immutable denormalized project_id with idx_thoughts_project_id

steps.name: Add immutable project_id column + index to label.schema.ts
criteria: labels table exports a projectId column ('project_id', uuid, notNull) referencing entities.id onDelete cascade
labels table declares a btree index 'idx_labels_project_id' on project_id
header comment reframes project_id as a denormalized immutable copy of the entity's project scope, dropping the old 'No project_id' note
column/index shape matches the reference pattern in chunk.schema.ts and relationship.schema.ts exactly
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: static schema metadata, not runtime behavior; backfill-correctness property compensates at step 01-03
steps.scenario_name: labels table exposes immutable denormalized project_id with idx_labels_project_id

steps.name: SQL migration: add nullable project_id, backfill, then constrain + index
criteria: new migration under apps/api/drizzle/migrations adds nullable project_id (uuid) to both thoughts and labels
migration backfills project_id from entities.project_id by joining each subtype row on subtype.id = entities.id, before any NOT NULL is applied
after backfill it sets project_id NOT NULL, adds FKs to entities.id onDelete cascade, and creates idx_thoughts_project_id and idx_labels_project_id
statement order is ADD nullable, UPDATE backfill, SET NOT NULL, ADD FK, CREATE INDEX, so pre-existing rows never violate NOT NULL
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: exact golden DDL; compensating fast-check property asserts every backfilled subtype.project_id equals its entity.project_id over generated fixtures
steps.scenario_name: Property: backfilled subtype project_id equals owning entity project_id for every row

phases.name: Service refactors: stamp project_id on write, filter it on read, collapse entity pre-lookups

steps.name: Refactor thoughts.service.ts to use denormalized project_id
criteria: create() stamps projectId = dto.projectId onto the thoughts insert within the same transaction as the entity insert
findByProject() filters eq(thoughts.projectId, projectId) and no longer innerJoins entities for scope
by-id methods (updateBody, findOne, setColor, clearColor, remove) resolve scope from the thought row's project_id, collapsing the entity-then-subtype N+1
assertOwnership still runs with the row's project_id before any mutation/read; NotFoundException still thrown for missing ids; remove() still deletes entities via cascade

unit tests are fast-check property-based with state-delta invariants (strict=true): create() yields project_id = supplied projectId; findByProject() returns only matching rows; single-example Jest only as // bypass: fallback
steps.scenario_name: Property: thoughts service stamps and filters project_id without joining entities for scope

steps.name: Refactor labels.service.ts to use denormalized project_id
criteria: create() stamps projectId = dto.projectId onto the labels insert within the same transaction as the entity insert
findByProject() filters eq(labels.projectId, projectId) and no longer innerJoins entities for scope
by-id methods (findOne, update, remove) resolve scope from the label row's project_id, collapsing the entity-then-subtype lookup
assertOwnership still runs with the row's project_id; NotFoundException still thrown for missing ids; remove() still deletes entities via cascade; service scope otherwise unchanged

unit tests are fast-check property-based with state-delta invariants (strict=true): create() yields project_id = supplied projectId; findByProject() returns only matching rows; single-example Jest only as // bypass: fallback
steps.scenario_name: Property: labels service stamps and filters project_id without joining entities for scope

steps.name: Verify uniform project_id scoping with no entities scope-join
criteria: all four scoped tables (thoughts, labels, relationships, chunks) expose a notNull project_id with a btree index, confirmed against their schema definitions
no read path in thoughts/labels/relationships/pipeline services innerJoins entities solely to resolve scope; entities is touched only for lifecycle or endpoint resolution
a full-project-graph read filters each table on its own project_id, yielding the same project_id across thoughts, labels, relationships, and chunks for a project
for every subtype row project_id equals its entity's project_id (re-asserts the 01-03 invariant on post-refactor reads); no method UPDATEs project_id, so it cannot diverge
contract test is fast-check property-based over generated multi-table fixtures: asserts cross-table uniformity and the no-scope-join invariant, unchanged() covering other slots, strict=true
steps.scenario_name: Property: project_id is uniform and immutable across all scoped tables with no entities scope-join

validation.notes: All six steps follow NN-NN format, criteria are testable and concrete. Phase decomposition is sound: Phase 01 (schema + safe migration ordering), Phase 02 (service stamping/filtering), Phase 03 (cross-table verification). No circular dependencies. Migration safety verified: ADD nullable -> UPDATE backfill -> SET NOT NULL -> ADD FK -> CREATE INDEX prevents NOT NULL violations. Service refactors correctly specify stamping, filtering, and scope-join collapse. Non-blocking: clarify bypass:fallback note in 02-01/02 and add rationale to 03-01 re-assertion.
